LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT BRUCE MCCORMACK AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR LACK OF INFORMED CONSENT AND THE THIRD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR FAILURE TO REFER TO A SPECIALIST. THESE ARE DUPLICATIVE CAUSES OF ACTION TO THE FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR MEDICAL NEGLIGENCE AND ADD NOTHING TO THE COMPLAINT BY WAY OF FACT OR THEORY. NO REASONABLE PROBABILITY THAT DEFECT COULD BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH TEN DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THE FOURTH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR MEDICAL BATTERY TO STATE FACTS THAT SUPPORT THE CLAIM. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD, REPORTER: MARIA LERTORA, CSR #11935

DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-13-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BRUCE MCCORMACK DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Appearances: Elia M. Deluca, Esq. of Lynch, Gilardi & Grummer (415) 397-2800, appearing for Defendant and Moving party; Carter M. Zinn, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 292-4100 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party. Argued and the Court adopted and modified its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff fails to plead lack of consent. Plaintiff consented to the removal of the schwannoma located in the brain near the base of the skull. Plaintiff allegations sound in professional negligence. (Counsel to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT

Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to strike granted without leave to amend. Plaintiff exceeded the scope of permissible amendment, and portions to be stricken include irrelevant and improper matter. CCP section 436. Order signed in open Court. AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Oct-22-2009 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT BRUCE MCCORMACK AMENDED DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. No appearances on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Demurrer sustained without leave to amend as to the second cause of action for lack of informed consent and the third cause of action for failure to refer to a specialist. These are duplicative causes of action to the first cause of action for medical negligence and add nothing to the complaint by way of fact or theory. No reasonable probability that defect could be cured by amendment. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the fourth cause of action for medical battery to state facts that support the claim. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) (302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Court Reporter: Maria Lertora, CSR #11935
